item.title: 【Serverless Glue Jobs at Scale: Where the Bottlenecks Really Are】大规模无服务器胶水工作：瓶颈到底在哪里
item.description:
【

At moderate volumes, AWS Glue feels almost effortless.

You increase workers. The job runs faster.

】

在中等音量下，AWS Glue 感觉几乎毫不费力。

您增加了工人。作业运行得更快。

item.title: 【How Data Integrity Breaks in Enterprise Systems and How Architects Prevent It】企业系统中的数据完整性如何被破坏以及架构师如何预防它
item.description:
【

In enterprise systems — especially in high-stakes domains like finance — data integrity is paramount. Data integrity means that information remains accurate, consistent, and trustworthy across the entire system lifecycle. When data integrity breaks down, organizations face flawed analytics, compliance violations, and costly decision errors. This article explores how data integrity can fail in enterprise environments and the architectural strategies engineers employ to prevent these failures.

Understanding Data Integrity in Enterprise Systems

Data integrity encompasses the completeness, consistency, accuracy, and validity of data. In practice, it means that data across all systems reflects reality without contradiction — for example, financial records balance out, employee information is consistent across HR and payroll, and reports can be trusted. Modern enterprise architectures often distribute data across multiple applications, which makes maintaining integrity challenging. A robust architecture must ensure that when one component changes data, all dependent components remain in sync or at least detect and reconcile discrepancies.

】

在企业系统中，尤其是在金融等高风险领域，数据完整性至关重要。数据完整性意味着信息在整个系统生命周期中保持准确、一致和可信。当数据完整性遭到破坏时，组织将面临分析缺陷、合规性违规和代价高昂的决策错误。本文探讨了数据完整性如何在企业环境中失败，以及工程师用来防止这些失败的架构策略。

了解企业系统中的数据完整性

数据完整性包括数据的完整性、一致性、准确性和有效性。 In practice, it means that data across all systems reflects reality without contradiction — for example, financial records balance out, employee information is consistent across HR and payroll, and reports can be trusted.现代企业架构通常将数据分布在多个应用程序中，这使得维护完整性变得具有挑战性。强大的架构必须确保当一个组件更改数据时，所有依赖组件保持同步或至少检测并协调差异。

item.title: 【Beyond the Chatbot: Engineering a Real-World GitHub Auditor in TypeScript】超越聊天机器人：用 TypeScript 设计真实的 GitHub Auditor
item.description:
【

AI agents have taken the world by storm and are making positive gains in all domains such as healthcare, marketing, software development, and more. The chief reason for their prominence lies in being able to automate routine tasks with intelligence. For example, in software development, stories and bugs have automated tracking in tools such as GitHub, Rally, and Jira; however, this automation lacks intelligence, often requiring engineers and project managers to triage them.

Using an AI agent, as you will learn in this article, smart triaging can be carried out using generative AI. AI agents can be developed using many techniques and in several programming languages. Python has been a leader in the AI and ML space, whereas JavaScript has been the undisputed king in web development and has been prominent in back-end development as well. 

】

人工智能代理席卷了世界，并在医疗保健、营销、软件开发等所有领域取得了积极进展。它们突出的主要原因在于能够利用智能自动执行日常任务。例如，在软件开发中，故事和错误已在 GitHub、Rally 和 Jira 等工具中自动跟踪；然而，这种自动化缺乏智能，通常需要工程师和项目经理对其进行分类。

使用AI代理，正如您将在本文中了解到的那样，可以使用生成式AI进行智能分类。人工智能代理可以使用多种技术和多种编程语言来开发。 Python 一直是 AI 和 ML 领域的领导者，而 JavaScript 则是 Web 开发领域无可争议的王者，并且在后端开发领域也表现突出。

item.title: 【The Clandestine Culprits: Unmasking Modern Web Security Misconfigurations (And Their Automated Nemeses)】秘密罪魁祸首：揭露现代网络安全错误配置（及其自动敌人）
item.description:
【

Executive Synopsis

In the labyrinthine ecosystem of contemporary web applications, security misconfigurations emerge as the most insidious — yet paradoxically preventable — vulnerabilities plaguing digital infrastructure. This deep-dive exposition illuminates the shadowy realm of misconfigured CORS policies, absent security fortifications, and recklessly exposed cookies through the lens of battle-tested detection methodologies. Leveraging industrial-grade arsenals like OWASP ZAP, SecurityHeaders.com, and sophisticated GitHub Actions orchestration, we architect bulletproof remediation strategies grounded in OWASP doctrine and forged in the crucible of high-stakes security incidents.

The Stealth Epidemic: When Configuration Becomes Your Digital Achilles’ Heel

Security misconfigurations don’t storm the gates with banners flying.

】

执行概要

在当代网络应用程序迷宫般的生态系统中，安全错误配置成为困扰数字基础设施的最阴险但又可预防的漏洞。这种深入的阐述通过久经考验的检测方法阐明了错误配置的 CORS 策略、缺乏安全防御措施以及鲁莽地暴露 cookie 的阴暗领域。利用 OWASP ZAP、SecurityHeaders.com 等工业级武器库和复杂的 GitHub Actions 编排，我们构建了基于 OWASP 原则并在高风险安全事件的严酷考验中锻造的万无一失的补救策略。

隐形流行病：当配置成为您的数字致命弱点

安全配置错误不会让横幅飘扬。

item.title: 【Extending Java Libraries with Service Loader】使用 Service Loader 扩展 Java 库
item.description:
【

When designing a Java library, extensibility is often a key requirement, especially in the later phases of a project. Library authors want to allow users to add custom behavior or provide their own implementations without modifying the core codebase. Java addresses this need with the Service Loader API, a built-in mechanism for discovering and loading implementations of a given interface at runtime.

Service Loader enables a clean separation between the Application Programming Interface (API) and its implementation, making it a solid choice for plugin-like architectures and Service Provider Interfaces (SPI). In this post, we’ll look at how Service Loader can be used in practice, along with its advantages and limitations when building extensible Java libraries.

】

设计 Java 库时，可扩展性通常是一个关键要求，尤其是在项目的后期阶段。库作者希望允许用户添加自定义行为或提供自己的实现，而无需修改核心代码库。 Java 通过 Service Loader API 满足了这一需求，这是一种用于在运行时发现和加载给定接口的实现的内置机制。

服务加载器实现了应用程序编程接口 (API) 与其实现之间的清晰分离，使其成为插件式架构和服务提供商接口 (SPI) 的可靠选择。在这篇文章中，我们将了解如何在实践中使用 Service Loader，以及它在构建可扩展 Java 库时的优点和局限性。

item.title: 【GitOps Secrets Management: The Vault + External Secrets Operator Pattern (With Auto-Rotation)】GitOps 秘密管理：Vault + 外部秘密操作员模式（带自动轮换）
item.description:
【

The GitOps community is deeply divided on secrets management. Some teams swear by Sealed Secrets, claiming Git should be the single source of truth for everything. Others argue that secrets have no business being in version control — encrypted or not. Both camps are partially right, but they’re missing the bigger picture: modern production environments need secrets that rotate automatically, scale across multiple clusters, and never touch your Git repository.

Why the Encrypted-in-Git Approach Is Dead

Let’s be honest about Sealed Secrets. When we first adopted it, the appeal was obvious: encrypt your secrets locally, commit them to Git, and let the cluster-side controller decrypt them. Simple, right?

】

The GitOps community is deeply divided on secrets management.一些团队信奉密封的秘密，声称 Git 应该是一切事物的唯一事实来源。其他人则认为，秘密与版本控制无关——加密与否。两个阵营都部分正确，但他们忽略了更大的图景：现代生产环境需要自动轮换、跨多个集群扩展的秘密，并且永远不会触及您的 Git 存储库。

为什么 Encrypted-in-Git 方法已经死了

让我们诚实地对待密封的秘密。当我们第一次采用它时，吸引力是显而易见的：在本地加密您的秘密，将它们提交到 Git，然后让集群端控制器解密它们。很简单，对吧？

item.title: 【Understanding Custom Authorization Mechanisms in Amazon API Gateway and AWS AppSync】了解 Amazon API Gateway 和 AWS AppSync 中的自定义授权机制
item.description:
【

AWS provides Lambda-based authorization capabilities for both API Gateway and AppSync, each designed to secure different API paradigms, highlighting their complementary roles and the confidence they inspire in combined security potential.

Amazon API Gateway positions Lambda authorizers as a security checkpoint between incoming requests and backend integrations — whether Lambda functions or HTTP endpoints. The authorizer validates credentials, executes custom authentication workflows, and produces IAM policy documents that explicitly grant or deny access. These policies guide API Gateway’s decision to forward or reject requests to backend services.

】

AWS 为 API Gateway 和 AppSync 提供基于 Lambda 的授权功能，每种功能都旨在保护不同的 API 范例，突出它们的互补作用以及它们在组合安全潜力方面激发的信心。

Amazon API Gateway 将 Lambda 授权方定位为传入请求和后端集成（无论是 Lambda 函数还是 HTTP 终端节点）之间的安全检查点。授权者验证凭证、执行自定义身份验证工作流程并生成显式授予或拒绝访问的 IAM 策略文档。这些策略指导 API Gateway 决定转发或拒绝对后端服务的请求。

item.title: 【Engineering an AI Agent Skill for Enterprise UI Generation】设计用于企业 UI 生成的 AI 代理技能
item.description:
【

Large language models have recently made it possible to generate UI code from natural language descriptions or design mockups. However, applying this idea in real development environments often requires more than simply prompting a model. Generated code must conform to framework conventions, use the correct components, and pass basic structural validation.

In this article, we describe how we built an Agent Skill called zul-writer that generates UI pages and controller templates for applications built with the ZK framework.

】

大型语言模型最近使得从自然语言描述或设计模型生成 UI 代码成为可能。然而，在实际开发环境中应用这一想法通常需要的不仅仅是简单地提示模型。生成的代码必须符合框架约定、使用正确的组件并通过基本的结构验证。

在本文中，我们将介绍如何构建名为 zul-writer 的代理技能 ，该技能可为使用 ZK框架。

item.title: 【Building an AI-First Enterprise: Multi-Agent Systems, DSLMs, and the New SDLC in 2026】打造人工智能优先企业：2026 年多代理系统、DSLM 和新 SDLC
item.description:
【

The company will use AI as an operational foundation rather than implementing it as a simple chatbot add-on in 2026. AI will evolve from standalone tools into an operational framework that includes multi-agent systems, specialized models, AI-based software development processes, enhanced security measures, data location tracking, and human–AI interface management under defined regulatory frameworks.

Agentic Workflows Become Normal

During 2024–2025, most teams used a single assistant connected to their product. In 2026, systems will evolve into multi-agent architectures in which multiple agents with specific capabilities collaborate on tasks — planner, researcher, executor, and verifier. Gartner identifies Multiagent Systems as a 2026 strategic trend, marking the maturation of many “chatbot-era” experiments.

】

到 2026 年，公司将使用人工智能作为运营基础，而不是将其作为简单的聊天机器人插件来实施。人工智能将从独立工具发展成为一个运营框架，其中包括多代理系统、专门模型、基于人工智能的软件开发流程、增强的安全措施、数据位置跟踪以及定义的监管框架下的人机交互界面管理。

代理工作流程变得正常

2024 年至 2025 年期间，大多数团队使用连接到其产品的单个助手。到 2026 年，系统将演变成多代理架构，其中具有特定功能的多个代理（规划者、研究人员、执行者和验证者）在任务上进行协作。 Gartner 将多代理系统确定为 2026 年战略趋势，标志着许多“聊天机器人时代”实验的成熟。

item.title: 【Why Reporting Is the Hardest Problem in Enterprise SaaS (And How We Solved It in Workday)】为什么报告是企业 SaaS 中最难的问题（以及我们如何在 Workday 中解决它）
item.description:
【

Reporting is often the most challenging problem in enterprise SaaS applications. It might seem surprising — after all, modern SaaS systems handle complex transactions, workflows, and integrations. Yet when it comes to extracting meaningful insights from all that data, many companies struggle. Instead of clarity, they end up with a patchwork of tools, conflicting numbers, and “final_final_v3.xlsx” files flying over email.

In my experience as a Workday enterprise architect, I’ve seen that reporting isn’t just a feature — it’s the endgame where all the hard problems of data and scale come home to roost. This article explores why reporting is so hard in enterprise SaaS and how we tackled it in Workday’s architecture.

】

报告通常是企业SaaS 应用程序中最具挑战性的问题。这可能看起来令人惊讶 - 毕竟，现代 SaaS 系统处理复杂的交易、工作流程和集成。然而，当涉及到从所有数据中提取有意义的见解时，许多公司都陷入困境。他们最终得到的不是清晰的工具、相互冲突的数字以及在电子邮件中飞扬的“final_final_v3.xlsx”文件。

根据我作为 Workday 企业架构师的经验，我发现报告不仅仅是一项功能 - 它是所有数据和规模难题最终解决的结局。本文探讨了为什么报告在企业 SaaS 中如此困难，以及我们如何在 Workday 的架构中解决这个问题。
